title: Word-Hub Grid Game — Chapter 6: Materials Around Us
description: A timed 3×3 (or 4×4) tap-to-match grid game. A prompt banner at the top alternates between two modes — 'Tap the definition of WORD' or 'Which word means DEFINITION'. Students tap the matching cell. Correct taps turn green; wrong taps shake red for 0.9 s. A 60-second countdown timer and score pill run in the header. A streak badge pops up on 3+ consecutive correct taps. At the end a summary card shows score, accuracy, and missed words for review.

additionalProps.title: Word-Hub Grid Game
additionalProps.subtitle: Read the prompt. Tap the matching cell as fast as you can!

words.definition: Any substance used to create or make an object.
The method of arranging objects into groups.
Having a shiny surface; characteristic of most metals.
A material through which things can be seen clearly.
A material through which objects can be seen but not clearly.
A material through which you are not able to see at all.
A material that dissolves completely when mixed in water.
A material that does not dissolve in water even after stirring.
Anything that occupies space and has mass.
A property that gives the quantity of matter in an object.
The space occupied by a substance or object.
A material that is difficult to compress or scratch.

schema.description: All agent-configurable content lives inside additionalProps.

title.description: Heading shown in the purple gradient header.

subtitle.description: Short instruction line beneath the heading.
subtitle.default: Read the prompt. Tap the matching cell as fast as you can!

gridSize.description: Grid dimension. 3 = 3×3 (9 cells), 4 = 4×4 (16 cells). Word pool must be >= gridSize².

words.description: Pool of vocabulary words. Must contain at least gridSize² entries (≥9 for 3×3, ≥16 for 4×4). Each round picks gridSize² words randomly from this pool.

word.description: The vocabulary word shown as a prompt or in the cell.

definition.description: Full definition shown as a prompt. Keep under 20 words for readability.

shortDef.description: ≤6 word summary displayed INSIDE the grid cell when mode is word_to_def.

game_phases.idle: Start screen with decorative preview grid and a Start Game button. Shows word count and timer duration.
game_phases.playing: Live game — timer bar shrinks, prompt alternates modes, 3×3 grid is tappable.
game_phases.summary: Full-screen gradient overlay with trophy, score, accuracy, question count, and missed-words review grid.

prompt_modes.word_to_def: Prompt says 'Tap the definition of: WORD'. Cells show shortDef strings.
prompt_modes.def_to_word: Prompt says 'Which word means: DEFINITION'. Cells show word strings.
prompt_modes.alternation: Mode is randomised independently for every new question — roughly 50/50 split.

correct_feedback: Grid reshuffles after 480 ms with new words and new prompt
wrong_feedback: Cell resets to idle after 900 ms
Missed word ID added to review list
ui_behaviour.streak_system: Every 3+ consecutive correct taps shows a '🔥 Nx Streak!' badge that animates up and fades.
ui_behaviour.grid_rotation: After each correct answer, a new set of gridSize² words is picked randomly from the full pool. The correct answer for the new prompt is always guaranteed to be in the grid.
ui_behaviour.timer_urgency: When timeLeft ≤ 10 s: timer bar turns red, timer text pulses red.
ui_behaviour.missed_words_set: Built from promptWord IDs at the time of each wrong tap. Deduplicated. Shown in summary card for review.

agent_readme.overview: WordGridGame is a timed vocabulary recall game. The student sees a 3×3 grid of word or definition chips and must tap the one matching the prompt banner at the top. It is fully self-contained — a single .tsx file with inline styles, Poppins font, and keyframes injected via useEffect following the MetalsNonmetalsUseMatcher pattern.
architecture_notes: Keyframes injected via useEffect → document.createElement('style') with id 'wg-kf-v1', cleaned up on unmount.
Three phases: idle (start screen) → playing (timer + grid) → summary (score + missed review).
pickNewPrompt() uses a history ring-buffer to prevent immediate repeat questions.
reshuffleGrid() always guarantees the correct answer is somewhere in the grid.
missedRef is a useRef<Set<string>> (not useState) to avoid stale closure in the timer interval.
Timer runs via setInterval in a useEffect dependent on phase === 'playing'.
setStepDetails() called once on mount — compatible with parent orchestrators.

additionalProps.title: Electricity Word Grid
additionalProps.subtitle: Tap the matching cell for each prompt!

words.definition: A material that allows electric current to flow through it easily.
A material that does not allow electric current to flow through it.
The force that pushes electric charges through a circuit.
The flow of electric charges through a conductor.
Opposition to the flow of electric current in a circuit.
A closed path through which electricity can flow.
A circuit where components are connected in a single loop.
A circuit where components are connected across multiple branches.
A safety device that melts to break the circuit if current is too high.

decision_tree.default_chapter6: Leave additionalProps empty — defaults to 12 Chapter-6 science words, 60 s, 3×3 grid.
decision_tree.custom_topic: Supply words[] with id/word/definition/shortDef. Minimum 9 entries for 3×3.
decision_tree.harder_challenge: Set gridSize to 4 and supply ≥16 words.

error_handling.insufficient_words: If words.length < gridSize², falls back to DEFAULT_WORDS (12 Chapter-6 entries).
error_handling.missing_shortDef: shortDef is required — keep it ≤ 6 words so it fits inside the grid cell.
error_handling.duplicate_ids: Each id must be unique; duplicates cause incorrect match evaluation.

instructions_for_student: Read the prompt at the top. Tap the matching cell as fast as you can. Green = correct, Red = wrong. Score as many as possible in 60 seconds!
